word: G5006 - ταλαντιαῖος - talantiaîos - tal-an-tee-ah'-yos - Weight of a talent

sections.paragraph:
- Original: ταλαντιαῖος

- Transliteration: Talantiaios

- Phonetic: tal-an-tee-ah'-yos

- Definition:

1. a weight or worth of a talent

a. a talent of silver weighed about 100 pounds (45 kg)

b. a talent of gold, 200 pounds (91 kg)

- Origin: from G5007

- TDNT entry: None

- Part(s) of speech: Adjective

- Strong's: From G5007; talent like in weight: - weight of a talent.
